summary: The public bounty API for Frantic bounty 97 returns inconsistent required artifact names. The narrative criteria and delivery example require evidence_json, but the top-level required_artifacts and delivery_contract.artifacts require status_json instead. A stranger following the public API contract could prepare the wrong artifact name for delivery.
expected_result: The required artifact names should be consistent across criteria.artifacts, required_artifacts, delivery_contract.artifacts, and the delivery example.
actual_result: criteria.artifacts and the delivery example include evidence_json, while required_artifacts and delivery_contract.artifacts include status_json and omit evidence_json.

observations.name: expected_vs_actual
observations.value: Expected artifact names to match across criteria, required_artifacts, delivery_contract, and delivery example; actual response mixes evidence_json and status_json.

observations.name: reproduction_steps
observations.value: Fetch the endpoint and compare bounty.criteria.artifacts, bounty.required_artifacts, bounty.delivery_contract.artifacts[].name, and bounty.criteria.deliveryExample.

observations.name: impact
observations.value: Workers or integrations can prepare the wrong delivery artifact name because the public contract is internally inconsistent.

reproduction_steps: Open https://gofrantic.com/v1/bounties/97 in a logged-out browser or fetch it with a normal public GET request.
Compare the artifact names across those public fields.
impact: This can cause failed or confusing deliveries because the public API and contract disagree on whether the machine-readable evidence artifact is named evidence_json or status_json.

duplicate_check.known_risk: Bounty 95 already has one delivered bug under review. The public event stream does not expose the submitted artifact contents, so this report may still need reviewer duplicate checking.
